name: Hadoop 2.x Ecosystem Architecture
chatResponse: I'll guide you through the Hadoop 2.x architecture, highlighting how YARN transformed Hadoop from a MapReduce-only framework into a multi-purpose distributed operating system.

steps.title: Decoupling with YARN
steps.content: In Hadoop 1.x, the **JobTracker** handled both resource management and job monitoring, creating a bottleneck. Hadoop 2.x introduced **YARN (Yet Another Resource Negotiator)**, which splits these duties. The **ResourceManager (RM)** acts as the ultimate authority for resource allocation, while the **ApplicationMaster (AM)** manages the lifecycle of a specific application instance.

steps.title: The Storage Foundation: HDFS
steps.content: The **Hadoop Distributed File System (HDFS)** remains the storage layer but gained significant improvements in 2.x, such as **HDFS Federation** (multiple NameNodes for scalability) and **High Availability** (Active/Standby NameNodes to prevent single points of failure). The **NameNode** manages metadata, while **DataNodes** store the actual blocks of data.

steps.title: Node-Level Execution
steps.content: Each worker node runs a **NodeManager (NM)**. Its job is to monitor resource usage (CPU, Memory) within **Containers** and report back to the ResourceManager. When a job runs, the RM allocates a container for the ApplicationMaster, which then requests additional containers for the actual tasks (MapReduce, Spark, etc.) across the cluster.

steps.title: Multi-Tenant Processing
steps.content: Because YARN is a general-purpose resource manager, Hadoop 2.x allows multiple processing engines to run simultaneously on the same data in HDFS. You can run **MapReduce** for batch processing, **Tez** for interactive queries, and **Spark** for streaming, all sharing the same cluster resources efficiently.

visualCode:
graph TD
    Client[Client Machine] -->|Submit Job| RM[YARN ResourceManager]
    Client -->|Metadata Request| NN[HDFS NameNode]

    subgraph Slave_Node_A [Worker Node 1]
        NM1[NodeManager]
        DN1[DataNode]
        AM[App Master Container]
        T1[Task Container]
    end

    subgraph Slave_Node_B [Worker Node 2]
        NM2[NodeManager]
        DN2[DataNode]
        T2[Task Container]
    end

    RM <-->|Heartbeat/Status| NM1
    RM <-->|Heartbeat/Status| NM2
    NM1 --- DN1
    NM2 --- DN2
    NN -.->|Block Reports| DN1
    NN -.->|Block Reports| DN2
    AM -->|Request Resources| RM
    AM -->|Launch Tasks| NM2

    style RM fill:#0f172a,stroke:#06b6d4,stroke-width:3px,color:#fff
    style NN fill:#0f172a,stroke:#ec4899,stroke-width:3px,color:#fff
    style AM fill:#1e293b,stroke:#10b981,stroke-width:2px,color:#fff
    style Default fill:none,stroke:#64748b,stroke-width:2px,stroke-dasharray: 5 5
